meaning: n.不称职的人,不适合的东西vt.与...不相称vi.不适合,不相称
examples: I have been made to feel a social and psychological misfit for not wanting children.
Once a squeaky, geeky misfit kid himself, his inventing career began at the age of 16, when he devised a dynamic lighting system that could respond to sound.
they didn't want me to be a social misfit.
In 1971, Guillermo del Toro, the film director, was a seven-year-old misfit in Guadalajara, Mexico.
His friend and former boss in the army, Steven Nadudvary, says Hintze was a misfit there.
